figures.caption: Figure 1: Motivation. Left: a vanilla DiT leaks “remove hand sanitizer” into surrounding pixels. Right: Oursfactorizes what (instruction) from where (mask), injects both via a per-block adapter modulated by a SpatialGate, and grounds the edit region automatically via a MaskPredictor — no user mask needed at deployment.
Figure 5: Qualitative comparison on MagicBrush dev. Ourspreserves unedited regions while applying the requested edit; baselines over-edit or miss the edit entirely.
Table 6: Full per-bucket metrics for the mask robustness sweep on MagicBrush dev (528 samples). Anchors in italics; perturbation rows feed an altered GT mask through variant G’s GT-mask path.

Figure 3: Mask robustness on MagicBrush dev. Each curve sweeps a perturbation family (erode/dilate/shift) applied to the GT mask before inference; dotted/dashed baselines mark the GT-mask oracle and the deployed MaskPredictor configuration. All three metrics degrade monotonically with no cliff, and the deployed predictor sits at roughly the boundary error of erode 16 px on L1.

Table 1: MagicBrush dev and Emu-Edit Test. L1, L2: pixel distance to GT (↓); CLIP-I, DINO: visual similarity to GT (↑); CLIP-T: text–image alignment with target caption (↑, = CLIPout on Emu-Edit; L2 not in Emu-Edit protocol). Baselines: SD-1.5–era and Emu-Edit aggregated as worstper-cell across (Sheynin et al., 2024; Zhao et al., 2024; Yu et al., 2024); 2024–2026 methods from EditMGT (Chow et al., 2025) Tab. 1, ACE from (Han et al., 2025). Ours= variant G deployed (MaskPredictor, no user mask). Best bold, second underlined.

Table 2: GEdit-Bench-EN (full, GPT-4.1 judge). G_SC = semantic correctness; G_PQ = perceptual quality; G_O = overall. Self-reported except: † reproduced by Step1X-Edit (Liu et al., 2025) (original predates GEdit); ‡ from Step1X-Edit GitHub leaderboard; § from ReasonEdit (Step1X-Image Team, 2025) Tab. 1 (same GPT-4.1+VIEScore protocol). FLUX.2 does not report GEdit; FLUX.1-Kontextdev is the BFL representative.

Figure 2: System overview. (a) Training: GT mask and VL hidden states are encoded into spatial and semantic tokens, fused, and injected via BlockAdapter+SpatialGate at every frozen DiT block; a MaskPredictor head is co-trained with a decoupled auxiliary loss. (b) Inference: the MaskPredictor produces the edit-region mask from source image and instruction alone — no user mask needed.

Table 3: Ablation on MagicBrush dev. Each row trained from the same init, sharing data/optimizer/resolution/inference; only the adapter-stack components differ. For controlled comparison across variants without a MaskPredictor, every row feeds the GT mask. Deployed config (G with MaskPredictor, no user mask) is in Table 1.
